summarization.a: The multi spectra chart shows Raman spectra with characteristic peaks corresponding to α-Fe₂O₃ and Fe₃O₄. Magnetite-related peaks at ~538 and ~668 cm⁻¹ are prominent alongside hematite modes.
summarization.b: The multi-spectra chart shows Raman spectra containing features from multiple iron oxide phases, including α-Fe₂O₃, γ-Fe₂O₃, and Fe₃O₄, indicating mixed-phase composition.
summarization.c: The multi-spectra chart compares Raman spectra measured at different temperatures. Peaks associated with iron oxides are observed together with substrate-related signals, allowing comparison of phase presence across conditions.

| ~412               | Medium             | α-Fe₂O₃             |
| ~498               | Low                | α-Fe₂O₃             |
| ~538               | High               | Fe₃O₄ (magnetite)   |
| ~668               | High               | Fe₃O₄ (magnetite)   |
| ~1320              | Medium             | α-Fe₂O₃             |

a.question: Which Raman peaks indicate the presence of magnetite?
a.answer: Peaks near 538 cm⁻¹ and 668 cm⁻¹ indicate magnetite (Fe₃O₄).
